summary: Frontend-only regression test of the updated HeroBanner on Landing page ('/'). All 9 requested checks pass at both desktop (1920x900) and mobile (390x844) viewports. New local streamer image /hero_streamer_a.png loads (HTTP 200), no old emergentagent.com URL is used. Countdown ticks every second, captions rotate every ~3s, bid toast rotates through 4 bidders. Mobile card is properly contained at 320px width, Clipping chip is display:none via 'hidden sm:inline-flex', before/after strip is BELOW the card at both breakpoints, no horizontal overflow (scrollWidth=390 vs innerWidth=390). CTA buttons navigate correctly to /customer/create and /marketplace. No console errors.
critical_code_review_comments: HeroBanner.jsx uses many setInterval timers (4 running concurrently). Consider using a single rAF loop or reduce update frequency of progress (currently every 90ms) to avoid unnecessary re-renders on mobile.

The before/after row reuses HERO_IMG twice (as both Before and After thumbs) — this is fine for a demo but visually confusing since Before/After look identical except for the lime overlay. Consider a distinct thumbnail for Before (e.g., grayscale VOD-style screenshot).
matchMedia hover:none check inside onMove disables tilt on touch devices correctly, but the cursor glare div still evaluates gx/gy state on desktop only (has hidden sm:block) — good. No fix needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: HeroBanner updated in commit under review is fully working. Local image /hero_streamer_a.png (626KB) serves correctly. Note: first-pass mobile bid-toast visibility check can false-negative because AnimatePresence mode='wait' briefly unmounts the element during transitions — poll over ~3s or use locator.wait_for to avoid flakiness. Similarly, when checking mobile 'Clipping chip hidden', use a scoped DOM query for the actual chip element (class contains 'hidden sm:inline-flex') rather than page.locator('text=Clipping') because 'Clipping' appears in the eyebrow copy and marketplace text.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Original two user-reported issues (poor AI image + cluttered mobile) are resolved: (1) new locally-hosted /hero_streamer_a.png loads with HTTP 200 and replaces the previous emergentagent-hosted image; (2) mobile layout is clean — card 320px wide, Clipping chip hidden below sm breakpoint, before/after row moved out of card into its own row below, no horizontal overflow.
